Task registry: manages Miroir task namespace.\n//!\n//! Phase 3: persistent backends (SQLite, Redis) alongside in-memory fallback.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse crate::task::{MiroirTask, NodeTask, NodeTaskStatus, TaskStatus, TaskFilter};\nuse crate::error::MiroirError;\nuse crate::scatter::NodeClient;\nuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\nuse crate::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// In-memory task registry implementation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    tasks: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, MiroirTask>>>,\n}\n\n/// Trait for node polling operations.\n/// Allows the task registry to poll nodes without tight coupling to HTTP client.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\npub trait NodePoller: Send + Sync {\n    /// Poll a single node for task status.\n    async fn poll_node_task(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        task_uid: u64,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<NodeTaskStatus, String>;\n}\n\n/// Node poller implementation using a NodeClient and Topology.\npub struct ClientNodePoller<C: NodeClient> {\n    client: Arc<C>,\n    topology: Arc<Topology>,\n}\n\nimpl<C: NodeClient> ClientNodePoller<C> {\n    /// Create a new node poller with the given client and topology.\n    pub fn new(client: Arc<C>, topology: Arc<Topology>) -> Self {\n        Self { client, topology }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl<C: NodeClient> NodePoller for ClientNodePoller<C> {\n    async fn poll_node_task(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        task_uid: u64,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<NodeTaskStatus, String> {\n        use crate::scatter::TaskStatusRequest;\n\n        let req = TaskStatusRequest { task_uid };\n        self.client\n            .get_task_status(node_id, address, &req)\n            .await\n            .map(|resp| resp.to_node_status())\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"{:?}\", e))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    /// Create a new in-memory task registry.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            tasks: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with the given node tasks.\n    pub async fn register_async(&self, node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        self.register_async_with_metadata(node_tasks, None, None).await\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with the given node tasks and metadata.\n    pub async fn register_async_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n        let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n            .as_millis() as u64;\n\n        let mut tasks = HashMap::new();\n        for (node_id, task_uid) in node_tasks {\n            tasks.insert(node_id, NodeTask {\n                task_uid,\n                status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            });\n        }\n\n        let task = MiroirTask {\n            miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n            created_at,\n            started_at: None,\n            finished_at: None,\n            status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            index_uid,\n            task_type,\n            node_tasks: tasks,\n            error: None,\n            node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n        };\n\n        // Insert the task\n        {\n            let mut registry = self.tasks.write().await;\n            registry.insert(miroir_id.clone(), task.clone());\n        }\n\n        // Spawn a background task to poll for status updates (simulated for Phase 2)\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id_clone = miroir_id.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            registry.poll_task_status_simulated(&miroir_id_clone).await;\n        });\n\n        Ok(task)\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with the given node tasks and metadata, with real node polling.\n    ///\n    /// This version takes a NodePoller implementation to actually poll nodes for status updates.\n    pub async fn register_with_poller<P: NodePoller + 'static>(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n        poller: Arc<P>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n        let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n            .as_millis() as u64;\n\n        let mut tasks = HashMap::new();\n        for (node_id, task_uid) in node_tasks {\n            tasks.insert(node_id.clone(), NodeTask {\n                task_uid,\n                status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            });\n        }\n\n        let task = MiroirTask {\n            miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n            created_at,\n            started_at: None,\n            finished_at: None,\n            status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            index_uid,\n            task_type,\n            node_tasks: tasks.clone(),\n            error: None,\n            node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n        };\n\n        // Insert the task\n        {\n            let mut registry = self.tasks.write().await;\n            registry.insert(miroir_id.clone(), task.clone());\n        }\n\n        // Spawn a background task to poll for status updates using real node polling\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id_clone = miroir_id.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            registry.poll_task_status_with_poller(&miroir_id_clone, poller).await;\n        });\n\n        Ok(task)\n    }\n\n    /// Get task by ID (async version).\n    pub async fn get_async(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Option<MiroirTask> {\n        let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n        tasks.get(miroir_id).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Delete a task from the registry.\n    pub async fn delete(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        Ok(tasks.remove(miroir_id).is_some())\n    }\n\n    /// Count total tasks in the registry.\n    pub async fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n        tasks.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Update the status of a task (async version for tests).\n    /// Automatically sets started_at when transitioning to Processing.\n    /// Automatically sets finished_at when transitioning to a terminal state.\n    pub async fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            // Set started_at when transitioning to Processing\n            if status == TaskStatus::Processing && task.started_at.is_none() {\n                task.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as u64);\n            }\n            // Set finished_at when transitioning to a terminal state\n            if matches!(status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled)\n                && task.finished_at.is_none() {\n                task.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as u64);\n            }\n            task.status = status;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update a node task's status (async version for tests).\n    pub async fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            if let Some(node_task) = task.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id) {\n                node_task.status = node_status;\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prune old tasks (in-memory only, for Phase 3 this will use durable storage).\n    pub async fn prune_old_tasks(&self, _cutoff_ms: u64) -> Result<usize> {\n        // In-memory implementation: no pruning in Phase 2\n        // Phase 3 will add durable storage and pruning\n        Ok(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Update the overall task status based on node task statuses.\n    pub async fn update_overall_status(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        let task = match tasks.get(miroir_id) {\n            Some(t) => t.clone(),\n            None => return Ok(false),\n        };\n\n        // Determine overall status from node tasks\n        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n        let mut any_failed = false;\n        let mut all_terminal = true;\n\n        for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n            match node_task.status {\n                NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                    all_terminal = false;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n                NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n                    any_failed = true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let new_status = if any_failed {\n            TaskStatus::Failed\n        } else if all_terminal && all_succeeded {\n            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n        } else if !all_terminal {\n            TaskStatus::Processing\n        } else {\n            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n        };\n\n        if new_status != task.status {\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                t.status = new_status;\n            }\n            Ok(true)\n        } else {\n            Ok(false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status.\n    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n    ///\n    /// Phase 2: Simulates node polling (tasks complete after ~500ms)\n    /// Phase 3: Will poll actual nodes via HttpClient using topology\n    async fn poll_task_status_simulated(&self, miroir_id: &str) {\n        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n        loop {\n            // Get the current task state\n            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n\n            let task = match task {\n                Some(t) => t,\n                None => return, // Task was deleted\n            };\n\n            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // In a real implementation, we would query the nodes here\n            // For Phase 2, we simulate status progression\n            // Phase 3 will add actual node polling via HttpClient\n\n            // Check each node task's status\n            let mut all_terminal = true;\n            for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n                match node_task.status {\n                    NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                        all_terminal = false;\n                    }\n                    NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {}\n                }\n            }\n\n            // For testing purposes, simulate tasks completing\n            // In production, this would poll actual nodes\n            if !all_terminal && delay_ms >= 500 {\n                // Simulate completion for testing\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &mut t.node_tasks {\n                        if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing) {\n                            node_task.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Update overall status\n                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n                    let mut any_failed = false;\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n                        match node_task.status {\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                                all_succeeded = false;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if any_failed {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n                    } else if all_succeeded {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                    } else {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                    }\n                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                            .unwrap_or_default()\n                            .as_millis() as u64);\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status, using real node polling.\n    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n    async fn poll_task_status_with_poller<P: NodePoller>(&self, miroir_id: &str, poller: Arc<P>) {\n        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n        loop {\n            // Get the current task state\n            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n\n            let task = match task {\n                Some(t) => t,\n                None => return, // Task was deleted\n            };\n\n            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Collect node IDs and task UIDs to poll\n            let node_polls: Vec<(NodeId, u64)> = task.node_tasks\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|(_, nt)| !matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed))\n                .map(|(node_id, nt)| (NodeId::new(node_id.clone()), nt.task_uid))\n                .collect();\n\n            if node_polls.is_empty() {\n                // All node tasks are terminal, update overall status\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n                    let mut any_failed = false;\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n                        match node_task.status {\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                                all_succeeded = false;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if any_failed {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n                    } else if all_succeeded {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                    } else {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                    }\n                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                            .unwrap_or_default()\n                            .as_millis() as u64);\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Poll each node for status\n            let mut node_statuses = HashMap::new();\n            for (node_id, task_uid) in &node_polls {\n                // Get node address from topology (would need topology here)\n                // For now, use a mock address - in production, this would come from the topology\n                let address = format!(\"http://{}\", node_id.as_str());\n\n                match poller.poll_node_task(&node_id, &address, *task_uid).await {\n                    Ok(status) => {\n                        node_statuses.insert(node_id.clone(), status);\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to poll node {} for task {}: {}\", node_id, task_uid, e);\n                        // On poll failure, keep the current status but mark for potential degradation\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Update node task statuses\n            {\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    for (node_id, status) in node_statuses {\n                        if let Some(node_task) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id.as_str()) {\n                            node_task.status = status;\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // Update started_at timestamp if moving to processing\n                    if t.status == TaskStatus::Enqueued {\n                        let any_processing = t.node_tasks.values().any(|nt| {\n                            matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Processing)\n                        });\n                        if any_processing && t.started_at.is_none() {\n                            t.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                                .unwrap_or_default()\n                                .as_millis() as u64);\n                            t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List tasks with optional filtering (Meilisearch-compatible).\n    pub async fn list_async(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        let guard = self.tasks.read().await;\n        let mut result: Vec<MiroirTask> = guard.values().cloned().collect();\n\n        // Apply status filter\n        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n            result.retain(|t| t.status == status);\n        }\n\n        // Apply index_uid filter\n        if let Some(index_uid) = &filter.index_uid {\n            result.retain(|t| t.index_uid.as_ref().map_or(false, |uid| uid == index_uid));\n        }\n\n        // Apply task_type filter\n        if let Some(task_type) = &filter.task_type {\n            result.retain(|t| t.task_type.as_ref().map_or(false, |ty| ty == task_type));\n        }\n\n        // Apply offset\n        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n            if offset < result.len() {\n                result = result[offset..].to_vec();\n            } else {\n                result.clear();\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Apply limit\n        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n            result.truncate(limit);\n        }\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Test helper: set error on a task (for testing failure scenarios).\n#[cfg(feature = \"test-helpers\")]\nimpl InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    pub async fn set_error_for_test(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: String, node_errors: HashMap<String, String>) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            t.error = Some(error);\n            t.node_errors = node_errors;\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub async fn set_timestamps_for_test(&self, miroir_id: &str, started_at: Option<u64>, finished_at: Option<u64>) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            if started_at.is_some() {\n                t.started_at = started_at;\n            }\n            if finished_at.is_some() {\n                t.finished_at = finished_at;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test helper: set the status of a specific node task.\n    pub async fn set_node_task_status_for_test(&self, miroir_id: &str, node_id: &str, status: NodeTaskStatus) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            if let Some(nt) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id) {\n                nt.status = status;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test helper: set the overall task status.\n    pub async fn set_task_status_for_test(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n            t.status = status;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stub TaskRegistry implementation for compatibility.\n/// This delegates to the async methods via tokio::task::block_in_place.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl crate::task::TaskRegistry for InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.register_async_with_metadata(node_tasks, index_uid, task_type).await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                Ok(registry.get_async(&miroir_id).await)\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(&miroir_id) {\n                    task.status = status;\n                }\n                Ok(())\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        let miroir_id = miroir_id.to_string();\n        let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(task) = tasks.get_mut(&miroir_id) {\n                    if let Some(node_task) = task.node_tasks.get_mut(&node_id) {\n                        node_task.status = node_status;\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(())\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.list_async(&filter).await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\n                Ok(rt) => rt,\n                Err(_) => return 0,\n            };\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.count().await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// TaskRegistryImpl — runtime-selected backend (Phase 3)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Task status as lowercase string for store serialization.\nfn status_to_str(s: TaskStatus) -> &'static str {\n    match s {\n        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse task status from store string.\nfn str_to_status(s: &str) -> TaskStatus {\n    match s {\n        \"succeeded\" => TaskStatus::Succeeded,\n        \"failed\" => TaskStatus::Failed,\n        \"processing\" => TaskStatus::Processing,\n        \"canceled\" => TaskStatus::Canceled,\n        _ => TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert a store `TaskRow` to a `MiroirTask`.\n///\n/// Per-node status is not persisted; all nodes are set to `Enqueued`.\n/// The GET /tasks/{id} endpoint polls nodes to reconstruct per-node status.\nfn row_to_task(row: crate::task_store::TaskRow) -> MiroirTask {\n    let node_tasks: HashMap<String, NodeTask> = row\n        .node_tasks\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n            (\n                node_id,\n                NodeTask {\n                    task_uid,\n                    status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                },\n            )\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirTask {\n        miroir_id: row.miroir_id,\n        created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n        started_at: row.started_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n        finished_at: row.finished_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n        status: str_to_status(&row.status),\n        index_uid: row.index_uid,\n        task_type: row.task_type,\n        node_tasks,\n        error: row.error,\n        node_errors: row.node_errors,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert a route `TaskFilter` to a store `TaskFilter`.\nfn filter_to_store(f: &TaskFilter) -> crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n    crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n        status: f.status.map(|s| status_to_str(s).to_string()),\n        index_uid: f.index_uid.clone(),\n        task_type: f.task_type.clone(),\n        limit: f.limit,\n        offset: f.offset,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Runtime-selected task registry backend.\n///\n/// - `InMemory`: Phase 2 behaviour, tasks lost on restart (default for sqlite without path).\n/// - `Sqlite`: persisted via `SqliteTaskStore`, survives restarts.\n/// - `Redis`: persisted via `RedisTaskStore`, required for multi-replica HPA.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n    Sqlite(Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>),\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    Redis(Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>),\n}\n\nimpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore::open(std::path::Path::new(path))?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Sqlite(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            tokio::runtime::Handle::current()\n                .block_on(crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore::open(url))\n        })?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Redis(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// In-memory fallback (no persistence).\n    pub fn in_memory() -> Self {\n        Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `SqliteTaskStore`, if this is the Sqlite variant.\n    pub fn as_sqlite(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(s) => Some(s),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `RedisTaskStore`, if this is the Redis variant.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn as_redis(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(r) => Some(r),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `TaskStore` trait object, if this is a persisted variant.\n    pub fn as_task_store(&self) -> Option<Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => None,\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(s) => Some(s.clone() as Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>),\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(r) => Some(r.clone() as Arc<dyn crate::task_store::TaskStore>),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with metadata (sync, compatible with `TaskRegistry` trait).\n    pub fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::register_with_metadata(\n                    r, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type,\n                )\n            }\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as i64;\n\n                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n                    created_at,\n                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks,\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid,\n                    task_type,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                };\n\n                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n\n                Ok(MiroirTask {\n                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n                    node_tasks: new_task\n                        .node_tasks\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n                        .collect(),\n                    error: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as i64;\n\n                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n                    created_at,\n                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks,\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid,\n                    task_type,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                };\n\n                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n\n                Ok(MiroirTask {\n                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n                    node_tasks: new_task\n                        .node_tasks\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n                        .collect(),\n                    error: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a task by Miroir ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::get(r, miroir_id),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update the overall task status.\n    pub fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_status(r, miroir_id, status)\n            }\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update a node task's status.\n    ///\n    /// For persistent backends this is a no-op for per-node status since it is\n    /// reconstructed from polling on each GET /tasks/{id}. The store only persists\n    /// the overall task status and node task UIDs.\n    pub fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_node_task(r, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n            }\n            // Per-node status is reconstructed from polling on each GET; not persisted.\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(_) => Ok(()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List tasks with optional filtering.\n    pub fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::list(r, filter),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Count total tasks.\n    pub fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::count(r),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set error on a task.\n    pub fn set_error(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // --- Alias methods (delegated to TaskStore) ---\n\n    /// Get an alias by name.\n    pub fn get_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<Option<crate::alias::Alias>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(None),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_alias(name)?.map(|row| crate::alias::Alias {\n                    name: row.name,\n                    kind: if row.kind == \"single\" {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Single\n                    } else {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Multi\n                    },\n                    current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                    target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                    generation: row.version as u64,\n                    created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                    updated_at: Some(row.created_at as u64), // Use created_at as fallback\n                }))\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_alias(name)?.map(|row| crate::alias::Alias {\n                    name: row.name,\n                    kind: if row.kind == \"single\" {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Single\n                    } else {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Multi\n                    },\n                    current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                    target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                    generation: row.version as u64,\n                    created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                    updated_at: Some(row.created_at as u64), // Use created_at as fallback\n                }))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new alias.\n    pub fn put_alias(&self, alias: &crate::alias::Alias) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let new_alias = crate::task_store::NewAlias {\n                    name: alias.name.clone(),\n                    kind: if matches!(alias.kind, crate::alias::AliasKind::Single) {\n                        \"single\".to_string()\n                    } else {\n                        \"multi\".to_string()\n                    },\n                    current_uid: alias.current_uid.clone(),\n                    target_uids: alias.target_uids.clone(),\n                    version: alias.generation as i64,\n                    created_at: alias.created_at as i64,\n                    history: vec![],\n                };\n                store.create_alias(&new_alias)\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let new_alias = crate::task_store::NewAlias {\n                    name: alias.name.clone(),\n                    kind: if matches!(alias.kind, crate::alias::AliasKind::Single) {\n                        \"single\".to_string()\n                    } else {\n                        \"multi\".to_string()\n                    },\n                    current_uid: alias.current_uid.clone(),\n                    target_uids: alias.target_uids.clone(),\n                    version: alias.generation as i64,\n                    created_at: alias.created_at as i64,\n                    history: vec![],\n                };\n                store.create_alias(&new_alias)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Delete an alias.\n    pub fn delete_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(false),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => store.delete_alias(name),\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => store.delete_alias(name),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List all aliases.\n    pub fn list_aliases(&self) -> Result<Vec<crate::alias::Alias>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(vec![]),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let rows = store.list_aliases()?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(|row| crate::alias::Alias {\n                    name: row.name,\n                    kind: if row.kind == \"single\" {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Single\n                    } else {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Multi\n                    },\n                    current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                    target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                    generation: row.version as u64,\n                    created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                    updated_at: Some(row.created_at as u64),\n                }).collect())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let rows = store.list_aliases()?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(|row| crate::alias::Alias {\n                    name: row.name,\n                    kind: if row.kind == \"single\" {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Single\n                    } else {\n                        crate::alias::AliasKind::Multi\n                    },\n                    current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                    target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                    generation: row.version as u64,\n                    created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                    updated_at: Some(row.created_at as u64),\n                }).collect())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl crate::task::TaskRegistry for TaskRegistryImpl {\n    fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::register_with_metadata(self, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type)\n    }\n\n    fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::get(self, miroir_id)\n    }\n\n    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::update_status(self, miroir_id, status)\n    }\n\n    fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::update_node_task(self, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n    }\n\n    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::list(self, filter)\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::count(self)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_register_creates_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert!(task.miroir_id.starts_with(\"mtask-\"));\n        assert_eq!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued);\n        assert_eq!(task.node_tasks.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_get_returns_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        });\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.unwrap().miroir_id, task.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_list_filters_by_status() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let (task1, _task2) = rt.block_on(async {\n            let t1 = registry.register_async(node_tasks.clone()).await.unwrap();\n            let t2 = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n            (t1, t2)\n        });\n\n        // Update task1 to succeeded - must be done within runtime context\n        let task1_id = task1.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task1_id) {\n                t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n            }\n        });\n\n        let filter = TaskFilter {\n            status: Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n            node_id: None,\n            index_uid: None,\n            task_type: None,\n            limit: None,\n            offset: None,\n        };\n\n        let tasks = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.list_async(&filter).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(tasks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(tasks[0].miroir_id, task1.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_update_node_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n\n tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        // Mark one node as succeeded, one as processing - must be done within runtime context\n        let task_id = task.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task_id) {\n                if let Some(nt) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(\"node-0\") {\n                    nt.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                }\n                if let Some(nt) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(\"node-1\") {\n                    nt.status = NodeTaskStatus::Processing;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Overall status should still be enqueued/processing\n        let updated = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.update_overall_status(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert!(updated);\n\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Processing);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_list_filters_by_index_uid() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let assert_eq!(tasks[0].task_type, Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_exponential_backoff_simulation() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 3);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        // Wait for task to complete (simulated exponential backoff: 25 + 50 + 100 + 200 + 400 = 775ms)\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(800)).await;\n        });\n\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded);\n        assert!(retrieved.finished_at.is_some());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_miroir_task_id_format() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert!(task.miroir_id.starts_with(\"mtask-\"));\n        // UUID test_in_memory_registry_survives_request_lifetime() {\n209\t    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n210\t\n211\t    // Register a task\n212\t    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n213\t    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1u64);\n214\t    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2u64);\n215\t\n216\t    let task = registry.register_async(node_tasks.clone()).await.unwrap();\n217\t    let miroir_id = task.miroir_id.clone();\n218\t\n219\t    // Simulate request ending (drop all references to task)\n220\t    drop(task);\n221\t\n222\t    // Sleep to ensure any potential cleanup would have run\n223\t    sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n224\t\n225\t    // Task should still be in registry\n226\t assert_eq!(retrieved.node_tasks.len(), 2);\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t#[tokio::test]\n234\tasync fn test_registry_survives_multiple_concurrent_requests() {\n235\t    let registry = Arc::new(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new());\n236\t\n237\t    // Simulate multiple concurrent requests creating tasks\n238\t    let mut handles = Vec::new();\n239\t\n240\t    for i in 0..10 {\n241\t        let reg = registry.clone();\n242\t        let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {\n243\t            let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n244\t            node_tasks.insert(format!(\"node-{}\", i), i as u64);\n245\t            node_tasks.insert(format!(\"node-{}\", i + 10), (i + 10) as u64);\n246\t\n247\t            reg.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap()\n248\t        });\n249\t        handles.push(handle);\n250\t    }\n251\t\n252\t    // Wait for all tasks to complete\n253\t    let mut miroir_ids = Vec::new();\n254\t    for handle in handles {\n255\t        let task = handle.await.unwrap();\n256\t        miroir_ids.push(task.miroir_id);\n257\t    }\n258\t\n259\t    // All tasks should be in the registry\n260\t    assert_eq!(miroir_ids.len(), 10);\n261\t    for miroir_id in &miroir_ids {\n262\t        let retrieved = registry.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n263\t        assert!(retrieved.is_some(), \"Task {} should be in registry\", miroir_id);\n264\t    }\n265\t\n266\t    // List should return all tasks\n267\t    let filter = TaskFilter::default();\n268\t    let all_tasks = registry.list_async(&filter).await.unwrap();\n269\t    assert!(all_tasks.len() >= 10, \"Should have at least 10 tasks\");\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n273\t// Additional: List and };\n308\t\n309\t    let succeeded_tasks = registry.list_async(&filter).await.unwrap();\n310\t    assert_eq!(succeeded_tasks.len(), 1);\n311\t    assert_eq!(succeeded_tasks[0].miroir_id, P2.5 Task ID reconciliation and /tasks endpoints verification tests.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - Fan-out to 3 nodes → all 3 `taskUid`s captured in one mtask\n//! - `GET /tasks/{mtask_id}` while all nodes are processing → `processing`\n//! - One node fails → status `failed`, error includes per-node breakdown\n//! - In-memory registry survives the request's own lifetime (Phase 3 makes it persistent)\n\nuse miroir_core::task::{NodeTaskStatus, TaskStatus, ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 1: Fan-out to 3 nodes → all 3 taskUids captured in one mtask\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_fan_out_to_3_nodes_captures_all_task_uids() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    // Simulate fan-out to 3 nodes, each returning a different taskUid\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 42u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 17u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 88u64);\n\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n    let miroir_task = rt.block_on(async {\n        registry.register_async_with_metadata(\n            node_tasks,\n            Some(\"test-index\".to_string()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        ).await\n    }).unwrap();\n\n    // Verify mtask ID format\n    assert!(miroir_task.miroir_id.starts_with(\"mtask-\"), \"Miroir task ID should start with 'mtask-'\");\n\n    // Verify all 3 node task UIDs are captured\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.node_tasks.len(), 3, \"Should capture all 3 node task UIDs\");\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.node_tasks.get(\"node-0\").unwrap().task_uid, 42);\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.node_tasks.get(\"node-1\").unwrap().task_uid, 17);\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.node_tasks.get(\"node-2\").unwrap().task_uid, 88);\n\n    // Verify metadata\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.index_uid, Some(\"test-index\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.task_type, Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test(flavor = \"multi_thread\")]\nasync fn test_task_registry_impl_captures_all_node_tasks() {\n    let registry = TaskRegistryImpl::in_memory();\n\n    // Register a task with 3 node tasks\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 100u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 200u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 300u64);\n\n    let miroir_task = registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_tasks,\n            Some(\"my-index\".to_string()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify all node tasks are preserved\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.node_tasks.len(), 3);\n    assert!(miroir_task.miroir_id.starts_with(\"mtask-\"));\n    assert_eq!(miroir_task.task_type, processing\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_get_task_while_nodes_processing_returns_processing() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    // Create a task with 3 nodes\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 3u64);\n\n    let task = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Update node tasks to processing using the public method\n    for node_id in [\"node-0\", \"node-1\", \"node-2\"] {\n        registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, node_id, NodeTaskStatus::Processing).await.unwrap();\n    }\n    registry.update_status(&task.miroir_id, TaskStatus::Processing).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Get the task\n    let retrieved = registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Processing);\n    assert!(retrieved.started_at.is_some());\n\n    // Verify all nodes are in test_get_task_while_one_node_still_enqueued_returns_processing() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 3u64);\n\n    let task = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Set mixed states: node-0 succeeded, node-1 succeeded, node-2 enqueued\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-0\", NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded).await.unwrap();\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-1\", NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded).await.unwrap();\n    // node-2 remains Enqueued\n\n    // Update overall status\n    registry.update_overall_status(&task.miroir_id).await.unwrap();\n\n    let retrieved = registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Processing);\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 3: One node fails → status failed, error includes per-node breakdown\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_one_node_failure_results_in_failed_status() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 3u64);\n\n    let task = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Set node-1 to failed\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-0\", NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded).await.unwrap();\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-1\", NodeTaskStatus::Failed).await.unwrap();\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-2\", NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded).await.unwrap();\n\n    let mut node_errors = HashMap::new();\n    node_errors.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), \"connection timeout\".to_string());\n    registry.set_error_for_test(&task.miroir_id, \"Task failed on 1 node\".to_string(), node_errors).await;\n    registry.update_status(&task.miroir_id, TaskStatus::Failed).await.unwrap();\n\n    let finished_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as u64;\n    registry.set_timestamps_for_test(&task.miroir_id, None, Some(finished_at)).await;\n\n    let retrieved = registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Failed);\n    assert!(retrieved.error.is_some());\n    assert!(retrieved.node_errors.contains_key(\"node-1\"));\n    assert_eq!(retrieved.node_errors.get(\"node-1\").unwrap(), \"connection timeout\");\n    assert!(retrieved.finished_at.is_some());\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_multiple_node_failures_aggregates_all_errors() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 3u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-3\".to_string(), 4u64);\n\n    let task = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Set multiple nodes to failed\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-0\", NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded).await.unwrap();\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-1\", NodeTaskStatus::Failed).await.unwrap();\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-2\", NodeTaskStatus::Failed).await.unwrap();\n    registry.update_node_task(&task.miroir_id, \"node-3\", NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded).await.unwrap();\n\n    let mut node_errors = HashMap::new();\n    node_errors.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), \"disk full\".to_string());\n request's own lifetime\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_in_memory_registry_survives_request_lifetime() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    // Register a task\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1u64);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2u64);\n\n    let task = registry.register_async(node_tasks.clone()).await.unwrap();\n    let miroir_id = task.miroir_id.clone();\n\n    // Simulate request ending (drop all references to task)\n    drop(task);\n\n    // Sleep to ensure any potential cleanup would have run\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n\n    // Task should still be in registry\n    let retrieved = registry.get_async(&miroir_id).await;\n    assert!(retrieved.is_some(), \"Task should survive request lifetime\");\n\n    let retrieved = retrieved.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(retrieved.node_tasks.len(), 2);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_registry_survives_multiple_concurrent_requests() {\n    let registry = Arc::new(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new());\n\n    // Simulate multiple concurrent requests creating tasks\n    let mut handles = Vec::new();\n\n    for i in 0..10 {\n        let reg = registry.clone();\n        let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n            node_tasks.insert(format!(\"node-{}\", i), i as u64);\n            node_tasks.insert(format!(\"node-{}\", i + 10), (i + 10) as u64);\n\n            reg.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap()\n        });\n        handles.push(handle);\n    }\n\n    // Wait for all tasks to complete\n    let mut miroir_ids = Vec::new();\n    for handle in handles {\n        let task = handle.await.unwrap();\n        miroir_ids.push(task.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    // All tasks should be in the registry\n    assert_eq!(miroir_ids.len(), 10);\n    for miroir_id in &miroir_ids {\n        let retrieved = registry.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some(), \"Task {} should be in registry\", miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    // List should return all tasks\n    let filter = TaskFilter::default();\n    let all_tasks = registry.list_async(&filter).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(all_tasks.len() >= 10, \"Should have at least 10 tasks\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Additional: List and filter tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_list_tasks_filters_by_status() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    // Create tasks with different statuses\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1u64);\n\n    let task1 = registry.register_async_with_metadata(\n        node_tasks.clone(),\n        Some(\"index-a\".to_string()),\n        Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    let task2 = registry.register_async_with_metadata(\n registry.update_status(&task1.miroir_id, TaskStatus::Succeeded).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Filter by succeeded status\n    let filter = TaskFilter {\n        status: Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n        node_id: None,\n        index_uid: None,\n        task_type: None,\n        limit: None,\n        offset: None,\n    };\n\n    let succeeded_tasks = registry.list_async(&filter).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(succeeded_tasks.len(), 1);\n    assert_eq!(succeeded_tasks[0].miroir_id, task1.miroir_id);\n\n    // Filter by enqueued status (task2 should be enqueued)\n    let filter_enqueued = test_list_tasks_with_limit_and_offset() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    // Create 5 tasks\n    let mut miroir_ids = Vec::new();\n    for i in 0..5 {\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(format!(\"node-{}\", i), i as u64);\n        let task = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n        miroir_ids.push(task.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    // List with limit=2, offset=0\n    let filter = TaskFilter {\n        status: None,\n        node_id: None,\n        index_uid: None,\n        task_type: None,\n        limit: Some(2),\n        offset: Some(0),\n    };\n\n    let page1 = registry.list_async(&filter).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(page1.len(), 2);\n\n    // List with limit=2, offset=2\n    let filter2 = TaskFilter {\n        status: None,\n        node_id: None,\n        index_uid: None,\n        task_type: None,\n        limit: Some(2),\n        offset: Some(2),\n    };\n\n    let page2 = registry.list_async(&filter2).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(page2.len(), 2);\n\n    // Pages should not overlap\n    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assert!(page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).next().is_none(), \"Pages should not overlap\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_count_returns_total_tasks() {\n    let registry = ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_exponential_backoff_polling_completes() {\n    let registry = completion (exponential backoff: 25 + 50 + 100 + 200 + 400 = 775ms)\n    sleep(Duration::from_millis(900)).await;\n\n    let retrieved = registry.get_async(&miroir_id).await.unwrap();\n tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_task_timestamps_are_set_correctly() {\n    let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1u64);\n\n    let task = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n    let miroir_id = task.miroir_id.clone();\n\n    // Initially: only created_at is set\n    let retrieved = registry.get_async(&miroir_id).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(retrieved.created_at > 0);\n    assert!(retrieved.started_at.is_none());\n    assert!(retrieved.finished_at.is_none());\n\n    // Move to processing\n    let started_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as u64;\n    registry.update_status(&miroir_id, TaskStatus::Processing).await.unwrap();\n    registry.set_timestamps_for_test(&miroir_id, Some(started_at), None).await;\n\n    let retrieved = registry.get_async(&miroir_id).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(retrieved.started_at.is_some());\n    assert!(retrieved.finished_at.is_none());\n\n    // Move to succeeded\n    let finished_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as u64;\n    registry.update_status(&miroir_id, TaskStatus::Succeeded).await.unwrap();\n    registry.set_timestamps_for_test(&miroir_id, None, Some(finished_at)).await;\n\n    let retrieved = registry.get_async(&miroir_id).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(retrieved.started_at.is_some());\n    assert!(retrieved.finished_at.is_some());\n    assert!(retrieved.finished_at.unwrap() >= operation = \"preflight\",\n393\t            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n394\t            \"node call started\"\n395\t        );\n396\t\n397\t        // 1. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n456\t        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n457\t\n458\t        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n459\t        tracing::debug!(\n460\t            target: \"miroir.node\",\n461\t            node_id = %node,\n462\t            address = %address,\n463\t            operation = \"preflight\",\n464\t            duration_ms,\n465\t            total_docs,\n466\t            \"node call completed\"\n467\t        );\n468\t\n469\t        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n470\t            total_docs,\n471\t            avg_doc_length,\n472\t            term_stats,\n473\t        })\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    fn get_task_status(\n477\t        &self,\n478\t        node: &NodeId,\n479\t        address: &str,\n480\t async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/');\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        // 1. Get total docs from Meilisearch stats endpoint\n        let stats_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", base, request.index_uid);\n        let stats_resp = self\n            .client\n            .get(&stats_url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Stats request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !stats_resp.status().is_success() {\n            // Index not found or node unreachable — return empty stats\n            return Ok(PreflightResponse {\n                total_docs: 0,\n                avg_doc_length: 0.0,\n                term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        let stats_body: Value = stats_resp\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse stats: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_docs = stats_body\n            .get(\"numberOfDocuments\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        // 2. Get DF for each term via search with limit=0\n        let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n        let search_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", base, request.index_uid);\n        for term in &request.terms {\n            let search_body = serde_json::json!({\"q\": term, \"limit\": 0});\n\n            let search_resp = self\n                .client\n                .post(&search_url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n                .json(&search_body)\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"DF search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if search_resp.status().is_success() {\n                let body: Value = search_resp\n                    .json()\n                    .await\n                    .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse DF response: {}\", e)))?;\n                let df = body\n                    .get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n                term_stats.insert(term.clone(), TermStats { df });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // 3. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n        //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            duration_ms,\n            total_docs,\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs,\n            avg_doc_length,\n            term_stats,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n usize,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub ranking_score: bool,\n    pub body: Value,\n    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n}\n\nimpl SearchRequest {\n    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n    ///\n    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n    /// data to apply pagination.\n    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n\n        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n\n        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n\n        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n\n        // Ensure query is set\n        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n        }\n\n        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n        }\n\n        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n        }\n\n        body\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct ScatterResult {\n    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n    pub partial: bool,\n    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\npub async fn plan_search_scatter(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n) -> ScatterPlan {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n        Some(g) => g,\n        None => {\n            return ScatterPlan {\n                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n            match selector.select(&replicas, chosen_group).await {\n                Some(node) => node,\n                None => replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n///\n/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\npub async fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n    index: &str,\n    floor: u64,\n    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n    replica_selector: Option<&ReplicaSelector>,\n) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n\n    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n        shard_count,\n        group,\n        rf,\n        query_seq,\n        index,\n        floor,\n        version_checker,\n    )?;\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        // Filter by version floor\n        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n                version >= floor\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        if eligible.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let selected = if let Some(selector) = replica_selector {\n            // Convert Vec<&NodeId> to Vec<NodeId> for selector\n            let eligible_owned: Vec<NodeId> = eligible.iter().map(|&n| n.clone()).collect();\n            match selector.select(&eligible_owned, chosen_group).await {\n                Some(node) => node,\n                None => eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()].clone()\n        };\n\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n    }\n\n    Some(ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    })\n}\n","numLines":300,"startLine":200,"totalLines":2144}}}
